title: vaccinium hasseltii var. sabuletrum
intro: Vaccinium hasseltii var. sabuletrum, also known as the Korean blueberry or Korean whortleberry, is a type of shrub native to East Asia that produces small, blue-purple edible berries. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. The health benefits of Vaccinium hasseltii var. sabuletrum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, the berries can be consumed fresh, used to make jams and preserves, or dried to be used as a tea or supplement.
